quoteFirst: “Just think, monsieur, I had no idea that we should go farther than Paris; and all that I saw of Paris was between twenty minutes past
quoteTime: seven and twenty minutes before nine
quoteLast: in the morning, between the Northern and the Lyons stations, through the windows of a car, and in a driving rain! How I regret not having seen once more Père la Chaise and the circus in the Champs Elysées!”
title: Around the World in Eighty Days
author: Verne, Jules, 1828-1905; Towle, George M. (George Makepeace), 1841-1893 [Translator]

quoteLast: KHAQQ from _Itasca_: “Will you please come in and answer on 3,105. We are transmitting constantly on 7,500 kilocycles. We do not hear you on 3,105. Please answer on 3,105. Go ahead.”
title: Daughter of the sky
author: Briand, Paul L., Jr., 1920-1986

quoteLast: The beginning of the part called “total” continues from 5:52 to 7:25 a.
title: The Chautauquan, Vol. 04, April 1884, No. 7
author: Chautauqua Literary and Scientific Circle; Chautauqua Institution; Flood, Theodore L., 1842- [Editor]
